title: लक्ष्मी द्वादश नाम स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री लक्ष्मी द्वादश नाम स्तोत्रम् ॥

श्रीः पद्मा कमला मुकुन्दमहिषी लक्ष्मीस्त्रिलोकेश्वरी
मा क्षीराब्धिसुता विरिञ्चिजननी विद्या सरोजासना।
सर्वाभीष्टफलप्रदेति सततं नामानि ये द्वादश
प्रातःकाले पठन्ति तेषु नियतं श्रीः प्रसन्ना सदा॥१॥

प्रथमं श्रीरिति प्रोक्तं द्वितीयं पद्ममेव च।
तृतीयं कमला प्रोक्तं चतुर्थं मुकुन्दमहिषी॥२॥

पञ्चमं लक्ष्मीरित्युक्तं षष्ठं त्रिलोकेश्वरी।
सप्तमं मेति विख्यातं अष्टमं क्षीराब्धिसुता॥३॥

नवमं विरिञ्चिजननी दशमं विद्या प्रकीर्तिता।
एकादशं सरोजासना द्वादशं सर्वाभीष्टफलप्रदा॥४॥

द्वादशैतानि नामानि लक्ष्मीदेव्याः प्रकीर्तयेत्।
सर्वसम्पत्करं नित्यं मङ्गलं सुखवर्धनम्॥५॥

॥ इति श्री लक्ष्मी द्वादश नाम स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Lakshmi Dwadash Nama Stotram ||

Shrih Padma Kamala Mukunda-mahishi Lakshmis-trilokeshwari
Ma Kshirabdhi-suta Virinchi-janani Vidya Sarojasana.
Sarvabhishta-phala-pradeti satatam namani ye dwadasha
Pratah-kale pathanti teshu niyatam Shrih prasanna sada.||1||

Prathamam Shrir-iti proktam dwitiyam Padmam-eva cha.
Tritiyam Kamala proktam chaturtham Mukunda-mahishi.||2||

Panchamam Lakshmeer-ity-uktam shashtham Trilokeshwari.
Saptamam Ma iti vikhyatam ashtamam Kshirabdhi-suta.||3||

Navamam Virinchi-janani dashamam Vidya prakirtita.
Ekadasham Sarojasana dwadasham Sarvabhishta-phala-prada.||4||

Dwadashaitani namani Lakshmi-devyah prakirtayet.
Sarva-sampat-karam nityam mangalam sukha-vardhanam.||5||

|| Iti Shri Lakshmi Dwadash Nama Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning: श्री, पद्मा, कमला, मुकुन्दमहिषी, लक्ष्मी, त्रिलोकेश्वरी, मा, क्षीराब्धिसुता, विरिञ्चिजननी, विद्या, सरोजासना और सर्वाभीष्टफलप्रदा - ये बारह नाम जो प्रातःकाल पढ़ता है उस पर श्री लक्ष्मी सदा प्रसन्न रहती हैं। इन द्वादश नामों के नित्य कीर्तन से सर्वसम्पत्ति, मंगल और सुख की वृद्धि होती है।
benefits: Invokes the twelve sacred names of Goddess Lakshmi
Invokes all desired wealth and prosperity when recited at dawn
Traditionally recited to invoke the constant grace of goddess lakshmi
Traditionally associated with auspiciousness and increases happiness
Traditionally recited for removal of poverty and financial difficulties
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Goddess Lakshmi, Lakshmi Dwadash Nama Stotram (लक्ष्मी द्वादश नाम स्तोत्रम्) stands as a devotional hymn of praise. Rooted in Padma Purana, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Regular recitation is believed to remove poverty and financial difficulties, grant all desired wealth and prosperity when recited at dawn, and bring auspiciousness and increase happiness. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Friday (Shukravar) and during Diwali and Varalakshmi Vrata. With 5 verses and a reading time of about 5 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Lakshmi Dwadash Nama Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Lakshmi Dwadash Nama Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The Lakshmi Dwadash Nama Stotram (लक्ष्मी द्वादश नाम स्तोत्रम्) is a short but enormously powerful five-verse hymn that enumerates twelve sacred names (dwadasha-nama) of Goddess Lakshmi, drawn from the Padma Purana tradition. The opening verse compactly recites all twelve names in a single shloka, and the subsequent verses enumerate each name with its sequential number (prathamam, dwitiyam, tritiyam, and so on) so the chanter can install each name in memory and meditation. The phalashruti declares that the goddess remains permanently pleased (Shrih prasanna sada) with whoever recites these twelve names in the morning, especially at brahma-muhurta. (Source: Padma Purana; classical Lakshmi stotra tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the twelve names of Lakshmi in this stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: The twelve sacred names of Lakshmi enumerated in this stotra are: (1) Shri — the auspicious one; (2) Padma — the lotus-dweller; (3) Kamala — born of the lotus; (4) Mukunda-mahishi — beloved queen of Vishnu (Mukunda); (5) Lakshmi — the bestower of all good fortune; (6) Trilokeshwari — sovereign of the three worlds; (7) Ma — the universal Mother; (8) Kshirabdhi-suta — daughter of the milk-ocean (Samudra-manthana origin); (9) Virinchi-janani — mother of Brahma (Virinchi, from whose navel-lotus Lakshmi proceeds); (10) Vidya — embodiment of all knowledge; (11) Saroja-asana — seated upon the lotus; (12) Sarvabhishta-phala-prada — bestower of all desired fruits. Each name installs a distinct aspect of the goddess. (Source: Lakshmi Dwadash Nama Stotram verses 2-4.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is morning (pratah-kala) recitation specifically prescribed?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 1 of the Lakshmi Dwadash Nama Stotram explicitly states: 'Sarvabhishta-phala-prada-iti satatam namani ye dwadasha pratah-kale pathanti teshu niyatam Shrih prasanna sada' — 'For those who recite these twelve names in the morning (pratah-kale), Goddess Shri remains permanently pleased.' Pratah-kala (the early morning, especially brahma-muhurta about 96 minutes before sunrise) is considered the most auspicious time for Lakshmi-prayer because it precedes the day's activities, sets the energetic foundation for prosperity, aligns the chanter with the freshly awakening solar energy, and corresponds to the Vedic ritual hour of morning sandhya. Pratah-kala chanting of these twelve names is said to ensure permanent (sada) and consistent (niyatam) Lakshmi-grace. (Source: Lakshmi Dwadash Nama Stotram verse 1.)

seoFaqs.question: When and on what occasions should the Lakshmi Dwadash Nama Stotram be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Lakshmi Dwadash Nama Stotram is most powerful on Friday (Shukravar — Lakshmi's day) when recited at brahma-muhurta or after morning bath. It is especially auspicious on Diwali (Kartik Amavasya — the supreme Lakshmi-puja night), the entire Diwali week from Dhanteras through Bhai Dooj, Varalakshmi Vrata (the Shravan / Sawan Friday before Purnima — celebrated across South India), Kojagiri / Sharad Purnima (Ashvin Purnima — when Lakshmi visits homes at midnight), Akshaya Tritiya, Margashirsha Thursdays (Thakali Margali Lakshmi puja), and every Friday morning as nityapaath. Newly married women, business-owners and students of vidya are especially advised to chant it daily. (Source: Padma Purana paddhati; Lakshmi vrata tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the benefits of reciting the Lakshmi Dwadash Nama Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti (verse 5) declares: Dwadashaitani namani Lakshmi-devyah prakirtayet, Sarva-sampat-karam nityam mangalam sukha-vardhanam — 'reciting these twelve names of Goddess Lakshmi grants all forms of prosperity, perpetual auspiciousness, and the increase of happiness.' Traditional benefits include: permanent grace of Lakshmi as guaranteed by verse 1; bestowal of every desired fruit (Sarvabhishta-phala-prada); attainment of all forms of wealth — Adi-Lakshmi, Dhana-Lakshmi, Dhanya-Lakshmi, Gaja-Lakshmi, Santana-Lakshmi, Vira-Lakshmi, Vidya-Lakshmi and Vijaya-Lakshmi (the Ashta-Lakshmi); growth in vidya through the name Vidya; removal of poverty; auspiciousness for marriage and family life; and the lasting joy (sukha-vardhanam) that comes from Mukunda-mahishi's grace. (Source: Lakshmi Dwadash Nama phalashruti.)

seoFaqs.question: How does the Lakshmi Dwadash Nama Stotram differ from the Lakshmi Ashtottara Shatanamavali?
seoFaqs.answer: Both are namavali-type prayers but with very different scope and use. The Lakshmi Dwadash Nama Stotram is a concise twelve-name hymn (5 verses, ~5 minutes) ideal for daily morning recitation, easy memorization, and instant invocation when time is short. The Lakshmi Ashtottara Shatanamavali (108 names) is a fuller archana-list designed for formal pushpa, kumkum and akshata offerings during Lakshmi puja, and takes considerably longer to recite. Devotees commonly recite the Dwadash Nama every morning as quick nityapaath and the Ashtottara Shatanamavali on Friday evening puja, Diwali, Varalakshmi Vrata and major Lakshmi festivals. Both grant Lakshmi-grace but serve distinct daily-versus-occasional ritual roles. (Source: classical Lakshmi stotra-paddhati.)
